summary: DHH announces a trip to Porto Alegre, Brazil with Mary to combine vacation with speaking about Rails at the International Free Software Forum. He asks readers for travel advice on restaurants, sightseeing, and safety concerns. The post reflects the early days of Rails evangelism when DHH was personally traveling to spread the framework internationally. It's a casual, personal blog entry typical of mid-2000s blogging culture.
key_points: DHH was speaking about Rails at the International Free Software Forum in Porto Alegre, Brazil
The trip involved twenty hours of travel from Copenhagen, showing the personal investment in promoting Rails globally
He solicits reader advice on tourism and safety, reflecting the interactive nature of early blogging
The casual mention of promising Jason (Fried) not to get killed shows the small, tight-knit nature of 37signals at the time
money_quotes: I'll be undertaking another twenty hours of traveling next Monday to get Mary and myself from Copenhagen to Porto Alegre, Brazil.
I've promised Jason not to get killed :)
key_insight: A brief personal travel post from Rails' early days, showing DHH's hands-on commitment to evangelizing the framework at international open source conferences.
